title: Ateco Straight Spatula, 4.5 Inch, Wood
description:
About this item
Small straight spatula by Ateco
Tapered ground stainless steel blade for even flexibility
Lovely natural wood handle; blade attached with 2 sturdy rivets
Blade measures 3/4 x 4 1/4-inches
Ateco - professional quality & quality design
reviews_5s:
Over all, a good product and easy to use. Feels sturdy, looks good, and definitely a good value!I like this style of spatula for peanut butter, salad dressing, etc. They are perfect for my application.I bought this Spatula for cutting cheese curds. The length and sturdiness, not to mention the rounded end, makes it a wonderful cheese curd cutting tool. But! When delivered, it comes with a major sticker on the end that is almost impossible to remove! I know you are all laughing at this, but I don't like to use the chemical sticker remover items on anything that I use for cooking. I used hot water, vegetable oil, vinegar and other assorted items before I finally got the last of the glue off the end of this Spatula! I know - I know! I am most likely being silly but there it is! But!!! Once free of the dreaded sticker and major glue, this Spatula is fantastic for cheese making and other things around the kitchen. The size is perfect! It is well made with a comfortable wooden handle. Easy to clean, (once the glue is gone). I would recommend hand washing because of the wooden handle (Dishwasher will ruin wooden handles).Had one of these that was about 60-70 years old and like new. As these things sometimes go, it turned up missing (I blame extraterrestrials). Naturally, I had to replace it. The Ateco fills the bill. What is so great about this item, aside from being exactly the right size for what I use it to do, is the construction. It has a full-tang with rivets through a quality wooden handle. Unlike ones with a plastic handle, the blade will never come loose. Because dishwasher detergents deplete the natural oils in wood, handwashing is the proper way to clean it. The only con is the label is pretty hard to remove from the blade. Ateco should bottle it for DIYers who need a glue that sticks to metal.I work in a pharmacy. I wanted something to represent that in my home museum keepsake world.This is a cool knife. It's perfect for getting mayo on burger buns (exactly what I purchased it for.) It's great with sandwiches for the same purpose.

I came across this knife by accident while looking for something else. It's a cool addition to the kitchen that I really like, but never would have set out to specifically get.

The look is very attractive to me. Like something a professional sandwich maker in a nice little mom & pop shop would use....... I'm thinking of a place in Burlington, Vermont on Church Street from when I was a kid. Already the good memories with this charming knife.This spreader is excellent for all foods - butter, jam, preserves, peanut butter & jelly. The only problem is that Amazon sealed the spreader in a package that requires cleaning the sealant off with mineral spirits,Very good. This is suitable for making small cakes. It feels good and not too light.Ordered 18AUG23, received 22AUG23 - I needed a good straight edge baking spatula for removing cakes and other food items from their pans. Looked on Amazon and settled for this 6-inch Ateco stainless steel spatula. It was housed in a fitted plastic bag and arrived in a larger box of items I had ordered. The Ateco sticker was REALLY stuck on the spatula tip as well as part of the plastic bag and it took me a few moments to get it off. I wonder what glue they use because it was a fight to the finish! Moving on, the spatula worked very well for my needs. I highly recommend this particular spatula and the seller.There I was, with my absurdly thin butter knife, trying to scrape up enough mayonnaise from the bottom of the jar, My hands were getting more mayo than the knife, and spreading it on bread seemed to take forever.
Disgusted, I took to the 'net to find something that might make putting together a sandwich a little easier. Then this little gem showed up.
I don't know what it's supposed to be for, but it's great for mayo (and, presumably, Miracle Whip). It's long enough to reach the bottom of most jars, and the long, flat blade is great for holding a good sized portion of mayo. Plus, it's easy as anything to spread that mayo around.
reviews_1s:
DON'T BUY THIS! This was the perfect size and shape spreader, but I can't remove the label. The label itself is up near the handle but adhered to the blade. I have tried to pry it off, soaked it in hot soapy water over night, used Dawn and GooGone directly on it under the tiny part I was able to pry up to see if that would break down the glue. Nothing. I could pull logos off cars easier than this.
I see that others have used Goo Gone and vinegar, etc. I don't want to ruin the utensil or cut myself. SOOOOO frustrating.Couldn’t get the stickiness from the tag to come off even after soaking it in soapy water over night. I’ve seen where other reviewers had this problem. The handle also stained my counter top. Luckily they are issuing me a refund, but obviously I would not recommend this product to anyone!The spatula looks find. Problem is I will never be able to use it. The sticker they put on it is nearly impossible to get off. And when you do get it off it leaves a big square of some of the strongest glue you have ever seen. I tried WD40 and GooGone and it still has remnants of the glue. WHY would a company put this type of sticker on the working part of a utensil for FOOD is beyond me! It goes to show the company appears to not care about food safety.

I DO NOT recommend this product, there are MANY equally good or better products in this niche.Returned the first to I got because I was sent off at instead of straight, order came today and they are off at again. Label tags says straight but it is clearly off set. The third picture is of straight (what I'm looking for) and the other is off set ( don't want). Hopefully they will get it right, if so I will update my review.This is an OK spatula, I bought a lot for a children's cooking class. Don't buy these unless you want to spend a lot of time trying to get the bar code sticker off the blade. I cannot figure out why they did this since they are in plastic bags- and I don't know what they used for glue. I have tried everything to get them off, now I am soaking. This is the most idiotic packaging mess I have ever encountered.Do not put the label directly on product. Too difficult to get off. Took me an hour to remove the glue.It’s both worth buyingDescription says wooden handle but I received a spatula with a plastic handle. Too light weight with this type of handle. Too much trouble to return, but disappointed that it’s not as described.the tip had a sticker with upc label attached couldn't get it off, glue stayed on the knife tip it was like superglued to it, put it in the dishwasher and the entire knife discolored and its suppose to be stainless steel and it is stamped that way, the whole knife is no good very thin and flimsyStop putting stickers that require 30+ minutes of intense scrubbing to remove. It makes me want to send it back…
